title: Unauthorized Steam Token Minting via Owner Privileges
reason: False positive: `Steam` is deployed via `new Steam(...)` inside `UpSwing`, so `Steam._UPS` is the `UpSwing` contract address, not the external deployer.

title: Centralized Admin Control with No Timelock
reason: This is a governance/trust assumption, not a distinct code vulnerability in the permissionless protocol logic.

title: Missing Zero Address Validation in setUNIv2
reason: Requires privileged misconfiguration by an allowed address; it does not create an independent permissionless exploit beyond trusted-admin control.

title: Division by Zero in amountPressure
reason: Only reachable if all UPS supply is voluntarily burned away; that is a terminal self-destruction scenario rather than a realistic independent protocol bug.

title: Reentrancy Vulnerability in _transfer
reason: The only external call is `sync()` on the configured pair; exploiting reentrancy would require a privileged actor to point `UNIv2` at a malicious contract, so this is not an independent permissionless issue.

title: Integer Overflow in amountPressure with High Leverage
reason: False positive: arithmetic uses `SafeMath`, and `leverage` is a `uint8`, so the proposed >1000 overflow path is not valid.

title: Missing Event Emissions for Critical Functions
reason: Transparency issue only; not a realistic protocol-security vulnerability.

title: Leverage Can Be Set to Zero Breaking Protocol
reason: Relies entirely on privileged admin action and is covered by the protocol's trusted-admin model rather than an exploitable bug.
